summary_one_line: An ICC profile whose embedded tone curve evaluates to y >= 131072.0 at some sample point, forcing the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An ICC profile carrying a tone-curve (Curve1) whose cmsEvalToneCurveFloat output for at least one of the 256 samples is >= 131072.0, so the comparison `if (y < 131072.0)` takes its false side. Reaching this requires a well-formed profile header (`acsp`/`mntr` magic, valid colorspace tag e.g. Lab/XYZ) plus curve-tag bytes whose decoded float magnitude is enormous.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach the curve evaluation and take the false branch 5580/5599 times (W shows F=5580 on the blocker), and the BYTE DIFF shows winners carrying alternate colorspace/tag bytes (4c 'L', 61 'a', 62 'b' at offsets 0x14-0x16) and a `63 'c'` at 0x2c absent in losers, i.e. a distinct parsed-profile configuration that drives the curve into the large-output regime.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds execute the curve evaluation but the false side is never taken (L shows T=15300 F=0 on the blocker); their bytes hold only the unmodified `58 'X' 59 'Y' 5a 'Z'` colorspace and zeros at the differentiating offsets, so the tone curve always evaluates below 131072.0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver and substitutes one comparison operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting the exact tag/curve bytes whose decoded float clears the 131072.0 magnitude threshold; the two winners (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) both add I2S over their non-I2S sibling (naive, value_profile), so the divergence is attributable to the I2S technique delta.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of a float comparison against 131072.0 driven by the tone-curve evaluation.
y is the output of cmsEvalToneCurveFloat over a parsed Curve, so the input must shape that curve.
Winner-resolving seeds carry distinct colorspace/tag bytes (Lab) where loser seeds keep XYZ, marking a different parsed-profile configuration.
Loser-blocking seeds take only the true side of the blocker (F=0), never reaching the large-output regime.
Both decisive winners differ from their loser only by the I2S technique.
cmplog/vpc add the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes a logged comparison operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2226 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 71deaabf621459a6` and observed one I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 of the 7-level ancestor chain; the tool's trailing signal confirms `1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 6`, characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the float-comparand threshold is reachable only via input bytes that have no pre-image in the comparison-operand stream (e.g. the large y is computed from buffer length, not from any input-byte sequence) should show cmplog/vpc NOT beating naive/value_profile; if I2S still wins there, the attribution to operand-substitution is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets (0x14-0x16 colorspace tag, 0x2c) are correlated with the winning configuration but the prompt does not directly show that those bytes are the comparison operand I2S planted to push y past 131072.0.
